changes_summary: Revised the plan to address both flags: (1) Added Phase 1 to implement the new gens-preserving allocation algorithm that the TDD-style tests expect, before removing the visibility gate. The old plan assumed the 70:30 split was correct and would be kept. (2) Enumerated ALL 6 test assertions affected by removing the visibility gate, computing exact expected values for each. The initial-state test (effectiveEditorHeight: 540→300, effectiveGensHeight: 300→300) and solo-editor test are now explicitly called out. Added a new stability test that proves heights don't change across visibility toggles.
questions: The degraded tier test expects editor=156, gens=94 for viewport=250 with idealEditor=225 and gens=300. This implies a ratio of 0.625 (5/8) that doesn't obviously derive from the known constants. Was there a specific formula in mind for this tier, or should we reverse-engineer it from the test expectation?

With coexistence heights always applied, the editor will be 300px instead of 540px in a 600px viewport when gens uses the default 300px height. That's a 44% reduction in editor space when solo. Is this acceptable, or should we consider a larger viewport threshold where coexistence kicks in?
success_criteria: Toggling editor or generations pane visibility produces zero change in effectiveEditorPaneHeight and effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight
No visible snap/jump in LayoutMainContent padding when a pane toggles
Side-pane vertical centering (useBottomOffset) transitions smoothly without jumps
PaneControlTab slide animation uses correct target dimensions from the start
All PanesContext tests pass — including the 3 previously-failing algorithm tests and the 2 updated visibility-gate tests
New stability test proves effective heights remain constant across all visibility toggle sequences
Downstream tests (LayoutMainContent.test.tsx, useBottomOffset.test.ts) pass without changes
assumptions: The working-tree tests are authoritative TDD specs — the code should be updated to match them, not vice versa
The acceptable trade-off is that panes are permanently at coexistence height even when solo (e.g., editor at 300px instead of 540px in a 600px viewport with default gens=300)
The degraded tier formula (viewport=250 → editor=156, gens=94) encodes a specific proportional split whose exact derivation will be confirmed during implementation against the test contract
Only the editor+generations vertical coexistence needs pre-computation; side panes (shots/tasks) use width and don't have this snap issue
The editorPaneHeight base value (90% of viewport) remains unchanged — the allocator constrains it as needed
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
